name: Little Gem
image_alt: Small tight heads of romaine-type lettuce
description: A compact mini romaine lettuce forming tight, upright heads with sweet, crunchy leaves and a buttery heart. One of the most flavorful lettuces available, combining romaine's crunch with butterhead sweetness.
history: A British heirloom, long grown in English kitchen gardens as 'Sugar Cos'. Gained widespread popularity in American specialty food culture in the early 2000s when it became a restaurant favorite.

good.reason: Different root depths, efficient use of space
Radishes mark rows and mature before lettuce crowds them

seasonal_quirks: Bolt-resistant for a romaine type but will eventually bolt in long days and heat. Partial shade in hot climates extends the season. Very cold-hardy — survives light frost.
harvest_cues: Harvest as individual leaves or as whole head when firm and upright — 4–6 inches tall. Heart should be pale yellow-green and tightly packed.
variety_notes: The most popular lettuce in high-end restaurant kitchens. Individual leaves are naturally cup-shaped — ideal for lettuce wraps. Single-serving size makes waste-free harvesting easy.
preservation: Store unwashed in refrigerator up to 1 week. Does not freeze.
